input.prompt:
I have a standard MSTest v2 project and I heard MSTest.Sdk simplifies things.
Can you help me convert my project to use MSTest.Sdk? Show me what the new
project file should look like.

data.content: With MSTest.Sdk, the SDK itself handles all three MSTest package references, so the project file becomes much simpler:

arguments.old_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.5.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to convert from the traditional MSTest v2 setup to MSTest.Sdk. With MSTest.Sdk, the project SDK changes from `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` to `MSTest.Sdk`, and you no longer need to manually reference the three MSTest packages (MSTest.TestFramework, MSTest.TestAdapter, Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk) — they're all handled by the SDK.

Let me update the project file.

arguments.old_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="2.2.10" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.5.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
The build succeeds but 2 tests fail. The `DataRow` tests pass `long` (1L, 2L) but the method parameter is `int`. The old MSTest v2 was lenient about this, but MSTest.Sdk (v3) is stricter. Let me check the test log for details.

The warning says: "DataRow argument type should match method parameter type. Mismatches occur at indices: (0, 0)"

The `DataRow` attributes use `1L` and `2L` (long values) but the method parameter is `int id`. I need to fix the parameter type or the DataRow values. Since the DataRow uses `long` literals, I should change the method parameter to `long`.


Let me look at the actual test failure logs to understand what went wrong.

data.message: Authorization error, you may need to run /login (Request ID: 3403:3675E8:DB564B:1026445:69F41279)
